rulings.Case Title: SUSSAN POURJAVID-GRANFORS VS. SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication And Joinder In Co-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Americas Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication And Joinder In Co-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Americas Motion For Summary Judgment Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action for premises liability and negligence and granted as to the third cause of action for products liability. Plaintiff Sussan Pourjavid-Granfors' objections to the declaration of Jorge Contreras are sustained. CCSF has not satisfied its summary motion burden as to either of the first two causes of action. CCSF's argument that it was not responsibe for maintaining the elevator is contrary to the legal rule that a principal who personally is not at fault is nonetheless liable for the torts of its agents committed within the course and scope of the agency. (Peredia v. HR Mobile Services, Inc. (2018) 25 Cal. App. 5th 680, 691-92). CCSF's argument that it lacked actual or constructive notice of the escalator being in a dangerous condition is based on inadmissible statements in Mr. Contreras' declaration. That declaration is largely based on information and belief, which is insufficient to show either personal knowledge or a required foundation. Paragraphs 5 and 6 of that declaration, which discuss Mr. Contreras' review of the work orders, are inadmissible per Lane v. City of Sacramento (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1345. CCSF has not presented any evidence regarding how the work orders are created and maintained and thus there is no basis to infer that they constitute a complete and accurate record of the problems with the escalator. CCSF also cites plaintiff's factually devoid response to special interrogatory 2. That interrogatory asks: "Please state in detail any and all facts that YOU believe support the allegations made in YOUR Complaint." While plaintiff did provide a factually devoid response, the court cannot infer based on that one general question that plaintiff cannot prove her case. The cited discovery is not sufficiently comprehensive to make that inference. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 107.) CCSF is entitled to summary adjudication on the products liability claim. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that CCSF did not design, distribute, or manufacture the escalator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SUSSAN POURJAVID-GRANFORS VS. SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 3, DEFENDANT THYSSENKRUPP ELEVATOR CORPORATION (ERRONEOUSLY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Defendant ThyssenKrupp Elevator Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action for premises liability and negligence and granted as to the third cause of action for products liability. Plaintiff Sussan Pourjavid-Granfors' objections to the declaration of Jorge Contreras are sustained. Mr. Contreras' statement that TKE did not control the premises is based on information and belief, which is insufficient to show either personal knowledge or a required foundation. In addition, his deposition states that TKE had the exclusive right to maintain the escalator. TKE's argument that it lacked actual or constructive notice of any defect in the escalator also fails because that argument is also based on inadmissible statements in Mr. Contreras' declaration. Paragraphs 5 and 6 of that declaration, which discuss his review of the work orders, are inadmissible per Lane v. City of Sacramento (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1345. TKE has not provided any evidence regarding how the work orders are created and maintained and no basis to infer that they constitute a complete and accurate record of the problems with the escalator. TKE fails to satisfy its summary motion burden based on plaintiff's discovery responses. TKE cites plaintiff's factually devoid response to special interrogatory 2. That interrogatory asks: "Please state in detail any and all facts that YOU believe support the allegations made in YOUR Complaint." The court cannot infer based on that one general question that plaintiff cannot prove her case. The cited discovery is not sufficiently comprehensive to make that inference. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 107.) In addition, CCSF propounded that interrogatory, so the response is even more attenuated as to TKE. TKE is entitled to summary adjudication on the products liability claim. KONE manufactured the escalator and plaintiff admits that all that TKE has done is provide replacement parts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY K LEE VS. HOSIE RICE LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Permit Late Payment Of Jury Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 5, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY LEE Motion To Permit Late Payment Of Jury Fees Plaintiff Anthony Lee's motion to permit late payment of jury fees is granted. Mr. Lee has shown good cause for relief from his failure to pay the jury fees on a timely basis. If Mr. Lee posts the required jury fees by October 17, 2018, Mr. Lee's right to a jury trial is reinstated. Defendants' argument that there are no jury triable issues raised by the parties' pleadings is not amenable to resolution on this motion and must be decided by the trial judge, not a pre-trial judge. "Contract construction does not always present a question of law. Interpretation of a written instrument becomes solely a judicial function only when it is based on the words of the instrument alone, when there is no conflict in the extrinsic evidence, or when a determination was made based on incompetent evidence. But when ... ascertaining the intent of the parties at the time the contract was executed depends on the credibility of extrinsic evidence, that credibility determination and the interpretation of the contract are questions of fact that may properly be resolved by the jury." Coyne v. De Leo (2018) 26 Cal. App. 5th 801, _, quoting City of Hope National Medical Center v. Genentech, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal. 4th 375, 395 (internal citations and quotation marks omitted)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Lee is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY K LEE VS. HOSIE RICE LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Defendants Answer To Plaintiffs Verified Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 5, DEFENDANTs SPENCER HOSIE, HOSIE RICE LLP and DIANE RICE's Motion For Leave To Amend Defendants Answer To Plaintiffs Verified Third Amended Complaint Defendants' motion for leave to file a first amended answer to the third amended complaint is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings at any time in a case, which is especially applicable to answers. Defendants have shown that the amended answer is consistent with their understanding of the parties' contractual relationship. Plaintiff Anthony Lee has not shown that the changes that defendants wish to make to their answer constitute sham allegations or that the motion should be denied due to judicial estoppel, unfair prejudice to him or defendants' asserted delay in filing this motion. Mr. Lee is not foreclosed by the ruling on this motion from arguing to the trial judge and/or jury that the amended answer conflicts with defendants' prior statements nor is he foreclosed from seeking a trial continuance to enable him to take discovery and file a summary motion regarding the amended answer if he deems appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: VIRAJ PATEL VS. BARRY EDWARD O'NEILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 3, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTS BARRY O'NEILL, K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that moving party has failed to establish that there are no alternative methods of verification of plaintiff's claimed income loss except by production of portions of his income tax returns. Such a showing is required by defendants' own authority. (King v. Mobile Home Rent Review, (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 1532.) Alternative methods include plaintiff's oral deposition, written interrogatories to plaintiff, demand for production of plaintiff's business records, subpoenas duces tecum to plaintiff's franchisor and customers, forensic audit and expert testimony. Moving party has failed to establish that such alternative methods are either unavailable or unavailing. Likewise, the Court finds, insofar as demonstrated here, that plaintiff did not waive his right to tax return privacy in his Workers Compensation proceeding. Instead, the moving papers show only that plaintiff, as Applicant, acknowledged that he had reviewed the Workers Compensation defendant's Listing of Exhibits which included plaintiff's 2015 and 2016 tax returns. There is no showing of whether or how the Workers Compensation defendant had obtained the tax returns, whether or not the tax returns were ever offered in evidence and whether or not plaintiff raised a privacy objection thereto. Accordingly, the motion to compel production will be denied. The Court finds no abuse of the discovery process in the making of this motion so sanctions will also be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 3, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF VIRAJ PATEL'S Motion To Compel Surveillance Materials (Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two, And To Special Interrogatories, Set Two) Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that the denial of limited production of documents would unfairly prejudice the party seeking discovery and would not infringe upon attorney-client communications or unconditionally protected attorney work product. Defendants are therefore ordered to produce all photographs and videotapes of plaintiff and any associated production, editing or alteration logs, but excluding surveillance logs (except as to production, editing, or alteration of photographs or videotapes of plaintiff Patel) and any communications whatsoever to or from counsel to defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VIRAJ PATEL VS. BARRY EDWARD O'NEILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defense Investigator Tava Miyata With Production Of Documents Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 3, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF VIRAJ PATEL'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defense Investigator Tava Miyata With Production Of Documents Duces Tecum Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that the denial of a limited deposition and limited production of documents would unfairly prejudice the party seeking discovery and would not infringe upon attorney-client communications or unconditionally protected attorney work product. Defendants are therefore ordered to produce Investigator Tava Miyata for oral deposition and production of documents. The deposition shall be limited to the physical circumstances of the Investigator's photographing or videotaping of plaintiff Patel, including date, time, place, manner, and editing or alteration by the Investigator, if any, of the photographs or videotapes and the authentication of the photographs, videotapes and associated logs and excluding any communications whatsoever to or from counsel to defendants. The documents to be produced shall be limited to all photographs and videotapes of plaintiff and any associated production, editing or alteration logs, but excluding surveillance logs (except as to production, editing or alteration of photographs or videotapes of plaintiff Patel) and any communications whatsoever to or from counsel to defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The prevailing party shall further submit a copy of the proposed order in Word format via email to the Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY A BONNICI VS. CHARLES MCMACKIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment [Ccp Sec 473(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 6, Plaintiff's Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment [Ccp Sec 473(B)] Plaintiff Timothy Bonnici's motion for relief from judgment is granted. The January 24, 2018 judgment is set aside based on the mandatory relief provisions of CCP section 473(b). Defendants Charles McMackin and Carroll Henry are entitled to an award of fees and costs of $5,000 to be paid by Mr. Bonnici's counsel Gino Molinari no later than December 31, 2018. While this is less than the amount claimed by defendants, the court's own review of the additional work caused by Mr. Molinari's admitted fault shows that the high range of reasonable fees and costs for that work is $5000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a single proposed order on Mr. Bonnici's motion for relief from judgment which includes all the substantive portions of the tentative ruling issued in advance of the September 6, 2018 hearing which were confirmed at that hearing without contest by either party and all the substantive portions of this tentative ruling. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY A BONNICI VS. CHARLES MCMACKIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 6, Demurrer To Complaint Defendants Charles McMackin and Carroll Henry's demurrer to all four causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Timothy Bonnici is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all four causes of action. The allegations in the complaint about the alleged defamatory statements are conclusory. The allegation that the defamatory statements refer to Mr. Bonnici's "conduct as a buyer and seller of jewelry and other items of value, with the intent of leading other sellers to believe false that" Mr. Bonnici and his associates "conduct their business in deceptive, illegal false and fraudulent manner" provides very little information about the content of the alleged defamatory statements and none about the persons to whom such statements were made. While Mr. Bonnici is not required to provide evidentiary facts, he does need to provide sufficient information for the court and defendants to know the gist of the alleged defamatory statements and to whom those statements were made. For example, do the alleged defamatory statements state that Mr. Bonnici lies to his prospective customers and/or sells goods that are not what he represents them to be and/or buys goods without the intention of paying for them? Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROBINSON ET AL VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 3, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC'S Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion seeks to preempt plaintiff Medina's extensive discovery. There is statutory authority for such a preemptive motion, but little explanatory case law. Neither party has supplied a copy of the operative Complaint, or even its date of filing, by which the relevance or materiality of the discovery requests might be measured. Similarly, neither party has explained the relationship to the subject matter of many far-reaching and complained-of requests. And neither party has explained with specificity the level of burden and expense which the disputed requests may entail. The Court also notes that Uber's responses to form interrogatories consist solely of objections with no substantive responses whatever, potentially thwarting the purposes of the Discovery Act. Similarly, the Court notes that plaintiff's simultaneous service of approximately 1200 requests potentially imposes burdens at a level not contemplated by the Discovery Act. It appears to the Court that inquiries concerning Maher Tamim and possible driver distraction due to transmissions to or from the Uber Driver App are reasonably related to the subject matter of the pending action or may lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and thus constitute proper discovery. Other inquiries are substantially broader, to the point of possible oppression, and bear less obvious indicia of proper discovery. Under the circumstances, and subject to argument and demonstration of good cause to the contrary, the Court will deny the motion for protective order as to any discovery request specifically referring to Maher Tamim and/or the Uber Driver App. The Court will, again subject to argument and demonstration of good cause to the contrary, grant the motion for protective order as to the remainder of the discovery requests in question without prejudice to further discovery into the subject matter thereof via discovery requests more narrowly tailored to the subject matter of the pending action. Sanctions will be denied. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. FAST WRAP RENO ONE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 7, PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Plaintiff Alten Construction, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication on the issue that defendant Fast Wrap One, LLC had a duty to defend Alten is denied. Alten has not satisfied its summary adjudication burden of showing that there was a claim against it for which a duty to defend applied. After the hearing on August 28, 2018 which continued the hearing on Alten's motion to allow the parties to submit further briefing, the court contacted the parties and requested that their briefing also address the issue of whether the San Francisco Unified School District made a claim asserted against Alten for which a duty to defend applied. Alten's position that the District's demand made in correspondence is sufficient to trigger a duty to defend lacks merit. While the issue does not appear to have been squarely decided, this court determines that the phrase "defend actions or proceedings" in Civil Code section 2778(4) means a duty to defend does not arise until an adjudicative or quasi-adjudicative proceeding of some kind such as a court or administrative agency proceeding, internal grievance, or arbitration has been initiated by a claimant. This construction of section 2778(4) comports with the language of that section, case law construing that section, other case law regarding a duty to defend, and common understanding of a duty to defend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Fast Wrap is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR GANDHI VS. WELLS FARGO & CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On And Motion To Compel Defendant Wells Fargo Equipment Finance, Inc. To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 3, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF HECTOR GANDHI'S Notice Of Hearing On And Motion To Compel Defendant Wells Fargo Equipment Finance, Inc. To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. Two Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff moves to compel production of documents. Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts were adequate. Defendant's objections as to relevance are insufficient because, in a multi-party case, discovery is not restricted to claims against a given co-defendant. Discovery from one defendant may often bear on claims against a co-defendant. Defendant's objections based on privilege are speculative and have no bearing as to the resolution of this motion. The Court will hear argument as follows as to whether Plaintiff has made an adequate showing of good cause for production of documents as to the following topics: 1. All loans referred to Evan Zalon by any Pacific Midwest BBG banker without limitation to NBD-related transactions. 2. All commissions paid to Evan Zalon without limitation to NBD-related transactions. 3. All loans closed by Evan Zalon without limitation to NBD-related transactions. 4. All loans to Allied Tank Lines. The Court will also hear argument as to: 1. The exact dates within which plaintiff claims lost commissions and within which Plaintiff's discovery would not be temporally overbroad. 2. Whether to order a further response to Request 21 other than adjustment of the period covered by defendant's response. 3. Whether the existing protective order alleviates any privacy concerns. 4. Whether sanctions should be awarded to either party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Both parties shall submit a proposed order in Word format via email to the Pro Tem prior to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC ET AL VS. BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Declaration Of Olivier J. Labarre
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 14, DEFENDANTs FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., and SKYGROUP REALTY, INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Default; Declaration Of Olivier J. Labarre Defendants BSI Financial Services, Inc., Fay Servicing, LLC, Anh Pham and Skygroup Realty, Inc.'s motion to set aside the default entered against them on August 10, 2018 is granted. The August 10, 2018 default is set aside as to all moving defendants per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP section 473(b). Because moving defendants' demurrer to the complaint was not taken off calendar until July 13, 2018, it was reasonable for defendants to believe that they did not need to respond to the first amended complaint until at least thirty days thereafter. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy favoring deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff 3H Renovation, LLC has not identified any legal prejudice to it by the granting of the motion. The California Supreme Court has stated that "very slight evidence" is required to set aside a default when, as here, the party seeking to do so acts promptly. (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal 3d 474, 478). Counsel for 3H's conduct in taking moving defendants' default without contacting counsel for the moving defendants to give them advance warning when the day before plaintiffs' counsel had received an email from moving defendants' counsel on a related subjects falls below the standards of civility and fair play expected of all counsel representing parties in California courts. Even if it was proper to take moving defendants' default, 3H should have stipulated to the setting aside of the default without need for this motion to have been filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: WENCESLAO VASQUEZ VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 9, DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant General Motors LLC's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations is granted with leave to amend. The only claim that supports punitive damages is the fraud claim. The civil penalties under the Song-Beverly Act are a distinct remedy. (Civil Code § 1794(c); Kwan v. Mercedes-Benz of N. America (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 174, 184.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WENCESLAO VASQUEZ VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2018 Line 9, DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant General Motors LLC's demurrer to the seventh cause of action for fraud by omission in the complaint filed by plaintiff Wenceslao Vazquez is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Mr. Vasquez has leave to allege his discovery of the alleged fraud with more particularity. Paragraphs 8-9 and 31 indicate that the defects manifested within a year of the purchase. Mr. Vasquez was arguably on inquiry notice at that time and the claim appears time barred per CCP section 338. "In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, '[a] plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.' [Citation] In assessing the sufficiency of the allegations of delayed discovery, the court places the burden on the plaintiff to 'show diligence'; 'conclusory allegations will not withstand demurrer.'" (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.) As explained in Cansino v. Bank of America (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472: "Because the discovery rule operates as an exception to the statute of limitations, if an action is brought more than three years after commission of the fraud, plaintiff has the burden of pleading and proving that he did not make the discovery until within three years prior to the filing of his complaint. To excuse failure to discover the fraud within three years after its commission, a plaintiff also must plead facts showing that he was not negligent in failing to make the discovery sooner and that he had no actual or presumptive knowledge of facts sufficient to put him on inquiry. To that end, a plaintiff must allege facts showing "the time and surrounding circumstances of the discovery and what the discovery was. Conclusory allegations will not withstand a demurrer. The discovery related facts should be pleaded in detail to allow the court to determine whether the fraud should have been discovered sooner." (Citations and internal quotation marks omitted). GM's substantive attack on the fraud by omission claim fails. Paragraphs 50-51 show a duty to disclose based on GM's exclusive knowledge and active concealment. (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651.) Paragraph 49 alleges that the defective cooling system posed a safety hazard. The economic loss rule argument does not apply if Mr. Vazquez is able to plead a viable non-time barred fraud claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for GM is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
